tests.title: Reading Part 4
tests.description: You are going to read an article from which five sentences have been removed. Choose from the sentences A–H the one which fits each gap (16–20). There are three extra sentences which you do not need to use.

content.title: An Evening Drama Workshop
content.text: An Evening Drama Workshop became one of the most interesting experiences Alex had during the year. Before the activity began, he felt nervous because he did not know the other participants. (16) [16]He worried that everyone else already knew each other well and would ignore him.[/16] Fortunately, the organisers encouraged everyone to introduce themselves at the start. || During the first few days, some activities were more difficult than Alex expected. He sometimes made mistakes and needed additional help from older students. (17) [17]Experienced participants explained the activities carefully and gave him useful advice whenever he felt confused.[/17] After a short time, Alex became much more confident asking questions and trying new ideas. || Later in the programme, participants worked together in teams to complete a practical challenge. Everyone shared ideas and solved problems together. (18) [18]By the end of the week, many participants continued talking and sharing ideas outside the organised activities.[/18] The organisers were pleased to see people becoming more comfortable working together. || When Alex returned home, he expected to forget many of the skills he had learned. Instead, he continued practising regularly every weekend. (19) [19]He even demonstrated some of the techniques to friends at school who were interested.[/19] His family were surprised by how enthusiastic he had become. || Now Alex recommends the programme to younger students. He believes the experience helped him become more independent and confident. (20) [20]Meeting people with similar interests was the thing he enjoyed most about the experience.[/20]

paragraphs.A: Several experienced students offered help whenever he needed support.
paragraphs.B: The organisers decided to change the timetable the following morning.
paragraphs.C: Alex had never visited the city before the programme started.
paragraphs.D: Some participants preferred working quietly on their own.
paragraphs.E: The weather became colder later that evening.
paragraphs.F: Many participants stayed in contact online after the programme ended.
paragraphs.G: Alex thought nobody would want to speak to him.
paragraphs.H: The activity centre closed earlier than expected that day.

questions.explanation: Alex worried people would ignore him.
It contrasts with Alex's increased confidence.
It contrasts with Alex enjoying shared interests.
